channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: NGX free float: Why a mandatory 20% rule would deepen Nigeria’s stock market
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: On the Nigerian Exchange (NGX), the current free float rules allow companies to list and remain listed even when they fall short of the 20% free float requirement, as long as the value of their free float meets a minimum naira threshold. The post NGX free float: Why a mandatory 20% rule would deepen

item.title: 2027 polls: NBC, INEC join forces against unethical broadcasting
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The National Broadcasting Commission (NBC) and the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) are set to introduce joint broadcast monitoring measures ahead of the 2027 general elections. The initiative would tackle unethical broadcast practices and promote responsible electoral coverage natio

item.title: World Cup: Ticket prices hit $60,000 ahead of Spain vs Argentina final
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Fans hoping to witness Sunday's FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina will have to dig deep into their pockets, with ticket prices on resale platforms climbing as high as $60,000 ahead of the highly anticipated showdown. item.title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Halts Loadings After Drone Strike
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast was halted after a drone strike.

item.title: Zino Aviation CEO: Why many licensed Nigerian pilots remain unemployed
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: In this exclusive interview with Nairametrics, Captain Zino Mario, Founder and CEO of Zino Aviation, shares his perspective on how Nigeria can strengthen its pilot training ecosystem, improve access to aviation careers and position itself as a regional centre for pilot training. The post Zino Aviati

item.title: 2027: INEC faces credible question as Amupitan confirms dead persons’ names on register
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general election, the credibility of the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, voters’ register has come under scrutiny. DAILY POST reports that this is following the recent revelation by the INEC chairman, Professor Joash Amupitan that names of the many dead persons wer

item.title: Elon Musk Loses $45.3bn As SpaceX Shares Slide After Aborted Starship Launch
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Elon Musk’s net worth slipped below $800 billion after SpaceX shares fell following an aborted Starship launch, wiping roughly $45.3 billion from his fortune in a single session. SpaceX stock dropped about 4.4% to near $125 on Friday, extending a five-day slide that has trimmed more than 14% from th

item.title: VIDEO: Terrorists released us before soldiers took us home – Rescued Oyo teacher
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A rescued Oyo teacher claims kidnappers released them before soldiers intervened. Learn about the 56-day ordeal and the journey home.
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-terrorists-released-us-before-soldiers-took-us-home-rescued-oyo-teacher/

item.title: Firm Nominated At Africa Financial Crime Awards For Compliance Work
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Africa licensed stablecoin-based infrastructure provider, Yellow Card, has been nominated for seven awards at the 2026 Morgans Governance, Risk Management and Compliance Financial Crime Awards. The nominations recognize the company’s work in governance, risk and compliance across more than 60 countr

item.title: World Cup: Olise beats Pele’s record after France’s 6-4 loss to England
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: France attacking midfielder, Michael Olise, on Saturday, beat late Brazilian football legend Pele’s record for assists in a single World Cup campaign. Olise achieved this feat after providing two assists in France’s World Cup third-place final 6-4 defeat to England. His two assists again

item.title: Sunday Spontaneous Post.
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: #TodayisforGratitudeandGrace#positivesunday #gratefulheart #sundaypeace #gentlevibes #faithandlight #sundaygrace #sundaycalmGood Morning. Today, let gratitude rise in your heart. Today is wrapped in grace, and the gentle presence of God.“This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad

item.title: Firm Target N2bn In Solar Financing With New Retail Hubs Nationwide
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Sterling Bank and StarTimes Nigeria have launched Sterling Solar Financing Hubs inside StarTimes retail outlets across the country, targeting N2 billion in renewable energy financing by the end of 2026. The move, which builds on over N600 million already disbursed in the past year, is aimed at helpi

item.title: Africa Prudential’s H1 2026 pre-tax profit rises 21.68% to N2.41 billion
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Africa Prudential Plc reported a pre-tax profit of N2.41 billion for H1 2026, representing 21.68% YoY growth from N1.98 billion in H1 2025, supported by higher interest income and growth in revenue from contracts with customers. The post Africa Prudential’s H1 2026 pre-tax profit rises 21.68% to N2.

item.title: The Dollarisation of Petrol
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The era of naira-priced petrol may be ending, as the federal government’s policy on naira for crude oil flops. What follows could reshape inflation, transport costs and the value of

item.title: Army dismantles illegal bunkering site, arrests six suspects in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion have dismantled a suspected illegal bunkering operation in the Orile General Area of Lagos State, arresting six suspects and recovering vehicles and petroleum products believed to be connected to the illicit activity. The operation was carried out on

item.title: Dangote Refinery Sees 75 Tanker Calls Monthly, Aims for 900–1,000 Annually
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Dangote Refinery’s offshore terminal has handled about 1,100 tanker calls since it began operations and currently sees roughly 75 tanker visits each month, the company said. The head of Port Infrastructure and Marine Operations at Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals, Captain Satendra Singh

item.title: Grid Upgrade Halts Power Supply To Bauchi, Gombe, Yola – NISO
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Independent System Operator (NISO) has announced a planned power interruption affecting Bauchi, Gombe, Yola and neighbouring communities to allow the Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) to fit an Optical Ground Wire (OPGW) on the Jos–Bauchi–Gombe 330kV transmission corridor. The outag

item.title: NNPP urges FG, NASS to slow down state police reform
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The New Nigeria People's Party (NNPP) has called on the Federal Government and the National Assembly to adopt a more cautious approach to the proposed establishment of state police, warning that the reform could be vulnerable to political abuse if implemented without adequate constitutional safeguar

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 42
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Dlamini Confident South Africa Can Dethrone Super Falcons
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Banyana Banyana goalkeeper Andile Dlamini is banking on experience to dethrone the Super Falcons of Nigeria, Completesports.com. Desiree Ellis’ side surrendered their continental crown to Nigeria at the 2024 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations. South Africa suffered a 2-1 defeat to the Super Falcons in th
